slides.title: Port Check Affiliate Program Quarterly Business Review
slides.headline: General performance review remains the primary QBR focus
slides.summary: A comprehensive year-over-year analysis of the Port Check affiliate program's performance, publisher dynamics, and strategic priorities to drive growth and optimise outcomes.

slides.title: Program Performance: Executive Summary
slides.summary: The Port Check Affiliate Program delivered mixed results in Reporting period not provided. While AOV grew N/A to - and conversion rate improved N/A, total sales declined N/A YoY driven by a N/A reduction in click volume. Total order value changed N/A to -. Full KPI breakdown follows on the next slides.

slides.title: KPI Summary Table: Volume, Conversion, Cost & ROI
slides.subtitle: Unified KPI breakdown vs prior year.
slides.footerNote: Conversion rate = Sales ÷ Clicks. AOV = Total Order Value ÷ Sales. ROI = Total Order Value ÷ Total Commission.

slides.title: Program-Level Breakdown: Volume & Conversion
slides.subtitle: Per-program view for selected request scope.

slides.title: KPI Highlights & Business Implications
bullets: Conversion Rate Movement: Conversion rate: data not available. Click volume changed N/A (-) while sales changed N/A (-), indicating a shift in converting traffic.
Sales Volume Movement: Total sales changed N/A (-). Click volume changed N/A (-). Largest declining publisher contribution requires confirmation from mover tables.
AOV Movement: AOV: data not available. Total order value changed N/A (-). AOV uplift was concentrated in a smaller set of higher-value publishers.
CPA Movement: CPA: data not available. Publisher commission changed N/A (-) year-over-year, so each conversion carried a changed acquisition cost.
ROI Trend: ROI: data not available. For every unit of commission in the current period, programme return moved from - to -, showing changed spend efficiency.

slides.title: Publisher Performance Overview
slides.subtitle: High-level summary of publisher activity, segmentation and YoY movement.
bullets: 12 brand-new publishers were activated, contributing €78,000 in combined OV.
Publisher concentration remains high: Publisher 1 and Publisher 2 account for €3,000 (300.0% of programme OV).
